name: payments-revenuecat
summary: RevenueCat in-app purchases / subscriptions — client side. Server entitlement (subscriptions table + webhook) already ships in the skeleton.

env.name: EXPO_PUBLIC_REVENUECAT_IOS_KEY
env.note: RevenueCat PUBLIC SDK key (appl_…). Safe on device. Omit in dev → SDK runs in Preview/sandbox and mocks offerings, so you can build the flow with no key.

env.name: EXPO_PUBLIC_REVENUECAT_ANDROID_KEY
env.note: RevenueCat PUBLIC SDK key (goog_…). Same as above for Android.

postInstall: npx expo install react-native-purchases  (needs a dev build — not Expo Go for real purchases)
Call configureRevenueCat() once at app start (e.g. in src/app/_layout.tsx), after sign-in pass the Supabase user id as appUserID
Server side already exists: supabase/functions/revenuecat-webhook + the subscriptions table (RLS read-only). Set the webhook secret to keep entitlement server-owned.
Replace src/features/payments/ui/paywall.tsx after the Claude Design pass
